summary: No release-risk defect was found on the exact reviewed HEAD. The one-command closure remains fail-closed, preserves legacy and rollback paths, and keeps merge, waiver, and material external effects under explicit human authority.

regression_guard.assessment: Exact-HEAD verification passed unit 288/288, integration 4/4, E2E 4/4 with AC 8/8, typecheck, and build. Focused review also confirmed legacy profile preservation, provider fallback, timeout and cancellation, managed-worktree HEAD enforcement, needs_changes repair replay, and current-HEAD final preparation. These assertions exercise behavior that would fail under stale-head acceptance, forged readiness, missing replay, or same-HEAD no-progress regressions.

path_surface_coverage.assessment: The reviewed paths cover the public execute run/status/resume/cancel boundary, managed-worktree execution, production run-session ownership, independent review and bounded repair, final current-HEAD Gate rebinding, traceability artifacts, and human-only authority. No reverse dependency from the run-session owner into the CLI layer was introduced.

release_risk_assessment.git_stability: HEAD remained 926227f945878299770448a03966c17dfa70158d throughout review; git diff --check origin/main...HEAD passed. The only raw dirty entry is the pre-existing .vibepro/config.json review-budget amendment, excluded from the reviewed user-content fingerprint.
release_risk_assessment.compatibility: Existing persisted runs retain their selected action profile. The legacy profile and explicit disable-autonomous-actions fallback remain available and observable. Production connector and independent-review contracts from the already merged predecessor work are reused rather than reimplemented.
release_risk_assessment.rollback: Rollback can select the legacy action profile or disable autonomous actions without schema migration or mutation of predecessor connector/review contracts.
release_risk_assessment.human_authority: The autonomous DAG contains diagnose, artifact preparation, implementation, verification, independent review, bounded needs_changes repair, and final preparation only. PR creation, merge, waiver, deployment, publication, and other material external effects remain explicit human actions.
release_risk_assessment.failure_containment: No-progress, actual-HEAD mismatch, malformed runtime result, authentication, quota, permission, timeout, cancellation, provider unavailability, Gate recheck, and CI-pending conditions produce typed fail-closed stops. Cancellation wins over stale completion.
release_risk_assessment.migration_and_external_surface: No API, database, authentication-schema, UI, network-protocol, or deployment migration is introduced by this Story.
release_risk_assessment.architecture_conformance: Target conformance passed; baseline violations improved from 81 to 80, delta -1, with no new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency.

readiness_boundary.assessment: This release-risk pass does not override the canonical Gate. The coordinator must record the current lifecycle result and rerun pr prepare on the authoritative HEAD. The 33-commit scope warning is a history-review/disposition item; the inspected scope is one coherent Story, contains no foreign work items, stays below the configured file limit, and is not marked unsafe for atomic override.
